hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 9th consecutive Uluka sprint. THINK→EXECUTE→SHIP completed in single session. Single-agent mode had no impact on sprint structure.

hypotheses.name: Wave parallelism helps
hypotheses.evidence: Exp 5: single-agent, no waves. 11m22s active time, 130K new-work tokens, 1226 LOC. Compare S6 (multi-agent): 10m28s, 212K tokens, 582 LOC. S9 produced 2x LOC with fewer tokens — but scope differs (extraction vs verification). No clear signal that delegation adds value at Uluka scale.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gate 2 (noUnusedLocals) caught unused 'basename' import in document-extractor.ts. Fixed immediately. 2 Python AST verifier tests failed due to routing priority change — caught by Gate 3 (vitest), diagnosed and fixed in same cycle.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: PM: Gherkin acceptance criteria written in THINK phase. Architect: no circular deps, side effects behind interfaces. Security: no eval/Function in new code. ProdEng: all new source files have test files, coverage +1.14pp. UX: --verbose diagnostics added. 5/5 H7 audit checks pass. PM Gherkin was concise (not full scenarios per feature) — partial.
